skill.name: Azure AI Text Translation SDK for Python
skill.description: Azure AI Text Translation SDK for real-time text translation, transliteration, language detection, and dictionary lookup. Use for translating text content in applications.
skill.summary: Python client library for Azure AI Translator service enabling real-time text translation, transliteration, and language operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and found 0 security issues (risk score: 0/100). The skill teaches proper Azure AI Translator SDK usage including secure credential handling via environment variables and Entra ID authentication. No malicious patterns or prompt injection detected.

content.user_title: Translate text with Azure AI Translator
content.value_statement: Need to add multilingual support to your Python applications? This skill provides production-ready patterns for Azure AI Translator including translation, transliteration, language detection, and dictionary operations.
actual_capabilities: Translate text to 100+ languages with a single API call
Detect source language automatically with confidence scoring
Perform dictionary lookup to find alternate translations and definitions
Handle HTML content while preserving markup structure
Identify sentence boundaries for better text segmentation
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Translator resource provisioned
API calls incur costs based on characters translated
Cannot translate images or audio (text only)
Dictionary features available for limited language pairs

use_cases.title: Multilingual Customer Support
use_cases.description: Automate translation of customer inquiries and support responses to serve global users in their native languages.
use_cases.target_user: Customer support teams handling international tickets

use_cases.title: Content Localization Pipeline
use_cases.description: Integrate translation into your content management workflow to publish documentation, websites, or marketing materials worldwide.

use_cases.title: Language Learning Applications
use_cases.description: Build educational tools that provide translations, dictionary definitions, and usage examples for language learners.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Translation
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate the following text to Spanish using Azure AI Translator: 'Hello, welcome to our service. How can we help you today?'
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick single-language translation for user-facing messages

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Language Translation
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this product announcement to French, German, Japanese, and Chinese simultaneously: 'We are launching a new feature next week that improves performance by 50 percent.'
prompt_templates.scenario: Distributing content across multiple regional markets

prompt_templates.title: Language Detection and Translation
prompt_templates.prompt: Detect the language of this customer message and translate it to English: 'Bonjour, j'ai un probleme avec ma commande. Pouvez-vous m'aider?'

prompt_templates.title: Dictionary Lookup with Examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the Spanish translations for the English word 'run' with usage examples showing different meanings (verb: to move quickly, noun: a series, etc.)
prompt_templates.scenario: Building language learning or reference tools

output_examples.input: Translate 'Good morning, how can I assist you?' to Spanish and French
output_examples.output: Spanish: 'Buenos dias, como puedo ayudarte?' | French: 'Bonjour, comment puis-je vous aider?'

output_examples.input: Detect language and translate: 'Danke fur Ihre Hilfe'
output_examples.output: Detected: German (confidence: 0.99) | Translation: 'Thank you for your help'

output_examples.input: Transliterate 'konnichiwa' from Latin to Japanese script

best_practices: Use Entra ID authentication instead of API keys when possible for better security
Batch multiple translation requests together (up to 100 texts) to reduce API calls
Specify the source language explicitly when known to improve translation accuracy
anti_patterns: Never hardcode API keys or credentials directly in your source code
Avoid translating sensitive data like passwords, tokens, or personal identifiers
Do not rely solely on automatic language detection for critical workflows

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure AI Translator?
faq.answer: You can use API key authentication with your Azure resource key and region, or use Entra ID (recommended) with DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity or service principal authentication.

faq.question: What languages are supported for translation?
faq.answer: Azure AI Translator supports over 100 languages for translation. Use the get_supported_languages method to retrieve the complete current list with language codes and native names.

faq.question: Can I translate HTML content without breaking the markup?
faq.answer: Yes, set text_type to 'html' in your translate request. The service preserves HTML tags while translating only the text content between tags.

faq.question: What is transliteration and when should I use it?
faq.answer: Transliteration converts text from one writing script to another (e.g., Latin to Japanese characters). Use it when you need to display text in a different script while preserving pronunciation.

faq.question: How do I handle profanity in translations?
faq.answer: Use the profanity_action parameter with values 'NoAction', 'Deleted', or 'Marked'. When marked, profanity is censored using asterisks or XML tags based on profanity_marker setting.

faq.question: Can I translate multiple texts in a single request?
faq.answer: Yes, pass an array of texts to the body parameter (up to 100 items). This is more efficient than making separate API calls for each text.
